question_text: A crane lifts a steel beam of weight 8000 N at a constant speed upwards. The cable pulling the beam experiences a tension force. The beam is also subject to air resistance of 200 N acting downwards. Calculate the tension in the cable and explain your reasoning using the principle of balanced forces.
correct_answer: The tension in the cable is 8200 N. Since the beam moves at constant speed (constant velocity), all forces must be balanced. The downward forces are weight (8000 N) plus air resistance (200 N) = 8200 N total. Therefore, the upward tension must equal 8200 N to balance these forces.
explanation: When the beam moves at constant speed upwards, it is in equilibrium, meaning the net force is zero. All downward forces (weight and air resistance) must be balanced by the upward tension force. Adding the weight and air resistance together gives the total downward force that must be balanced, so the tension equals 8200 N.
